title: Iran's Assembly of Experts Formally Installs Mojtaba Khamenei as Supreme Leader
detail: Iran's Assembly of Experts formally appointed Mojtaba Khamenei, 56, son of the late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, as Iran's new supreme leader. President Masoud Pezeshkian, IRGC leadership, and senior officials pledged allegiance. The younger Khamenei maintains close ties with the Revolutionary Guard and signals a continuation of hard-line policies. Trump called the appointment 'unacceptable.'

media.caption: Iran's Assembly of Experts Formally Installs Mojtaba Khamenei as Supreme Leader

title: Pentagon Names 7th American Killed — Sgt. Benjamin Pennington
detail: The Pentagon identified Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington, 26, of Glendale, Kentucky, as the seventh U.S. service member killed in the conflict. Pennington died March 8 from wounds sustained March 1 at Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia while assigned to the 1st Space Brigade. The six previously confirmed U.S. deaths occurred at a U.S. military installation in Kuwait in the conflict's opening hours.

media.caption: Pentagon Names 7th American Killed — Sgt. Benjamin Pennington

title: IDF Destroys IRGC Air Force HQ, Space Command, and 50 Ammo Bunkers
detail: Israeli forces destroyed the headquarters of the IRGC Air Force and the IRGC Space and Satellite Command — the command center for Iran's ballistic missile operations. Additionally, 50 bunkers storing ammunition were destroyed, along with a Basij unit base, the Internal Security Forces headquarters, an IRGC Ground Forces compound, and the fuel depot at Kish Island airport. The Iranian navy ship Bandar Lengeh was also destroyed in the port of Bandar Abbas.

media.caption: IDF Destroys IRGC Air Force HQ, Space Command, and 50 Ammo Bunkers

title: Senior IRGC Officials Eliminated in Israeli Strikes
detail: Israeli strikes eliminated two senior IRGC figures: Abolghasem Babaeian, chief of the Supreme Leader's military office, and Haj Ali Hashem, a senior IRGC official based in Isfahan. A separate Israeli strike on a hotel in Beirut's Ghobeiry district targeting a meeting of five Quds Force commanders caused a major explosion.

media.caption: Senior IRGC Officials Eliminated in Israeli Strikes

title: Bahrain's Bapco Energies Declares Force Majeure After Refinery Strike
detail: Bahrain's state oil company Bapco Energies declared force majeure on its refinery operations after an Iranian UAV struck the Bapco refinery complex, disrupting output. At least 32 people, including children, were injured in Iranian strikes on the island of Sitra, south of Manama. Force majeure allows suspension of contractual delivery obligations during extraordinary events.

media.caption: Bahrain's Bapco Energies Declares Force Majeure After Refinery Strike

title: Brent Crude Briefly Tops $119 per Barrel; Markets Plunge
detail: Brent crude oil surged to $119.50 per barrel — the highest since Russia's 2022 invasion of Ukraine — before settling at $112.98 as fears of supply disruptions intensified. Global stock markets plunged. U.S. national gasoline prices reached approximately $3.45 per gallon. The VIX volatility index remained highly elevated. Trump characterized the energy price spike as 'a small price to pay' to eliminate Iran's nuclear threat.

media.caption: Brent Crude Briefly Tops $119 per Barrel; Markets Plunge

title: Iran Launches Fresh Missile and Drone Barrages on Gulf States and Israel
detail: Iran launched a new wave of missile and drone attacks on Gulf countries and Israel. Qatar reported about a dozen explosions as air defenses intercepted incoming missiles. Saudi Arabia intercepted UAVs headed for the Shaybah oil field. Fujairah, UAE reported a fire at an oil facility struck overnight. Two civilians were killed in Al-Kharj, Saudi Arabia from a falling projectile. NATO forces intercepted a second Iranian missile headed toward Turkey.

media.caption: Iran Launches Fresh Missile and Drone Barrages on Gulf States and Israel

title: Lebanon Calls for Ceasefire Talks; France Leads Mediation
detail: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun announced Lebanon's readiness to renew negotiations with Israel to stop the escalation, committing to the November 2024 ceasefire framework and demanding Israel halt strikes on Lebanese territory. Prime Minister Nawaf Salam said 'ideas are on the table,' primarily from France. France is leading international mediation efforts for Lebanon, while the Lebanese government formally banned Hezbollah's military activities — a significant political move.

media.caption: Lebanon Calls for Ceasefire Talks; France Leads Mediation

title: Trump: War 'Very Far Ahead of Schedule,' May End Soon
detail: President Trump suggested the war with Iran might be concluding soon, saying: 'We're getting very close to finishing that too,' and characterizing the operation as 'very far ahead of schedule.' Trump and Defense Secretary Hegseth both indicated the U.S. will set the 'terms of surrender' with Iran, with Israel's Netanyahu part of the decision but Washington having the final say. No ceasefire discussions were disclosed.

media.caption: Trump: War 'Very Far Ahead of Schedule,' May End Soon

title: Human Rights Watch Reports Israel Used White Phosphorus in Southern Lebanon
detail: Human Rights Watch reported that Israel unlawfully used white phosphorus munitions in southern Lebanese residential areas, causing severe burn injuries to civilians. The use of white phosphorus in civilian-populated zones is widely regarded as a violation of international humanitarian law. Over 400 people have been killed and 500,000 displaced in Lebanon since the start of the conflict.

media.caption: Human Rights Watch Reports Israel Used White Phosphorus in Southern Lebanon
